reasoning: Today is June 22, 2025, leaving only until July 1 for both chambers to pass the exact same budget reconciliation bill. The Senate has not yet passed its version, having delayed the floor action and 'vote-a-rama' to the week of June 22. The Senate has proposed changes to the bill (which the House originally passed 215-214). Even if the Senate manages to quickly pass its version by late June, the House would have a virtually non-existent legislative window to either adopt the Senate's changes without losing a single vote or to go to conference, resolve differences, and pass a final identical report in both chambers before July 1. The sheer procedural delay intrinsic to Senate budget reconciliation and House-Senate negotiations makes passage by July 1 highly improbable.
